methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 59.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Rendezvous Robotics is a pre-seed stage space and aerospace company founded by Ariel Ekblaw, Phil Frank, and Joe Landon. The firm has raised $3M to date. The broader Space & Aerospace sector includes 6 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, though direct same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables in this subsector remain sparse.

Given data constraints, only the stage-progression method is applicable. Using total capital raised ($3M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Pre-Seed to next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $8M. This method assumes normalized step-up dynamics within the sector. Peer-median triangulation was not viable; no sufficiently narrow peer set of three or more same-stage or adjacent-stage Space & Aerospace companies exists in the dataset. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 59.8x across 5 tickers) cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures, which private companies rarely provide.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6M to $9M, centered at $8M. This range reflects ±15% variance around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of private peer benchmarks at the pre-seed stage, lack of revenue or operating metrics to stress-test the stage multiplier, and limited visibility into capital efficiency relative to later-stage Space & Aerospace ventures. Additional fundraising data or operational milestones would strengthen conviction in this range.
